      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scanNextToken_preserves_prefix (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_next : Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok (some s')) (n : Nat) (h_n : n ≤ s.tokens.size) (h_inv : SimpleKeyAbove s n) (i : Nat) (h_bound : i < n) :
      s'.tokens[i] = s.tokens[i]

      scanNextToken preserves existing token prefix below n.

      For any index i < n ≤ s.tokens.size, tokens[i] remains unchanged, provided SimpleKeyAbove s n holds (so scanValuePrepare's setIfInBounds never overwrites tokens below n).

      Dispatch-level SimpleKeyAbove maintenance #

      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.dispatchStructural_maintains_simpleKeyAbove (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchStructural s c = Except.ok (some s')) (n : Nat) (_h_n : n ≤ s.tokens.size) (h_inv : SimpleKeyAbove s n) :
      SimpleKeyAbove s' n
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.dispatchFlowIndicators_maintains_simpleKeyAbove (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchFlowIndicators s c = Except.ok (some s')) (n : Nat) (_h_n : n ≤ s.tokens.size) (h_inv : SimpleKeyAbove s n) :
      SimpleKeyAbove s' n
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.dispatchBlockIndicators_maintains_simpleKeyAbove (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchBlockIndicators s c = Except.ok (some s')) (n : Nat) (_h_n : n ≤ s.tokens.size) (h_inv : SimpleKeyAbove s n) :
      SimpleKeyAbove s' n
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.dispatchContent_maintains_simpleKeyAbove (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchContent s c = Except.ok s') (n : Nat) (_h_n : n ≤ s.tokens.size) (h_inv : SimpleKeyAbove s n) :
      SimpleKeyAbove s' n
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scanNextToken_maintains_simpleKeyAbove (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_next : Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok (some s')) (n : Nat) (h_n : n ≤ s.tokens.size) (h_inv : SimpleKeyAbove s n) :
      SimpleKeyAbove s' n

      scanNextToken maintains the SimpleKeyAbove invariant.

      After scanNextToken, all simple keys (current and stacked) still have tokenIndex ≥ n. This follows from:

      • saveSimpleKey sets tokenIndex = st.tokens.size ≥ n (fresh key) or leaves key unchanged / clears it.
      • Dispatch functions either don't touch simpleKey, clear it (possible = false), push/pop from the stack (preserving the invariant), or restore from stack (which was saved when the invariant held).
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scanLoop_preserves_tokens (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (fuel : Nat) (tokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)) (n : Nat) (h_n : n ≤ s.tokens.size) (h_inv : SimpleKeyAbove s n) (h : Scanner.scanLoop s fuel = Except.ok tokens) (i : Nat) (h_bound : i < n) :
      ∃ (h_bound' : i < tokens.size), tokens[i] = s.tokens[i]

      scanLoop preserves existing tokens (prefix preservation below n).

      When scanLoop succeeds, tokens below index n remain unchanged, provided the SimpleKeyAbove s n invariant holds at the loop entry and is maintained by each scanNextToken step.

      Proof strategy:

      • Base case (fuel = 0): scanLoop returns error, contradiction
      • Inductive case: split on scanNextToken result
        • If none: uses unwindIndents + emit (both proven to preserve prefix)
        • If some s': uses IH + scanNextToken_preserves_prefix + maintains_simpleKeyAbove
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scanLoop_increases_tokens (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (fuel : Nat) (tokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)) :
      Scanner.scanLoop s fuel = Except.ok tokens → tokens.size ≥ s.tokens.size + 1

      scanLoop preserves or increases token count.

      When scanLoop succeeds, the resulting tokens have at least as many tokens as the input state, plus the streamEnd token (so at least +1).

      Proved by structural induction on fuel. Base case (fuel = 0) is contradiction. Success path (scanNextToken = none) uses unwindIndents_adds_tokens + emit_tokens_size. Recursive path uses IH + scanNextToken_adds_tokens.

      §1 Token Envelope Properties #

      The scanner's scan function (Scanner.lean:1968-1989) emits streamStart as its first action (line 1970) and streamEnd as its final action (line 1987) before returning the token array.

      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scan_produces_at_least_two (input : String) (tokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)) (h : Scanner.scan input = Except.ok tokens) :
      tokens.size ≥ 2

      The scan function produces at least 2 tokens.

      Proof strategy: The scan function:

      1. Emits streamStart (Scanner.lean:1970)
      2. Loops through scanNextToken
      3. Emits streamEnd (Scanner.lean:1987) before returning

      Since emit appends exactly one token each time, we have at least 2.

      After refactoring to structural recursion: This proof becomes tractable via induction on fuel. The key facts are:

      • mk' produces 0 tokens (mk'_tokens_empty)
      • emit adds 1 token (emit_tokens_size)
      • scan does: mk' → emit streamStart → scanLoop → emit streamEnd
      • Therefore: 0 + 1 (streamStart) + 1 (streamEnd) = at least 2
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scan_first_is_streamStart (input : String) (tokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)) (h : Scanner.scan input = Except.ok tokens) (h_size : tokens.size > 0) :
      tokens[0].val = YamlToken.streamStart

      The first token produced by scan is always streamStart.

      Proof strategy: Scanner.lean:1970 shows s := s.emit .streamStart as the first token emission. The subsequent loop only appends tokens (via emit), never modifying or removing earlier tokens. Array indexing at position 0 thus retrieves this initial streamStart token.

      Note: Complete proof requires tracking array invariants through the loop. Empirically validated on all test inputs (§4).

      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scan_last_is_streamEnd (input : String) (tokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)) (h : Scanner.scan input = Except.ok tokens) (h_size : tokens.size > 0) :
      tokens[tokens.size - 1].val = YamlToken.streamEnd

      The last token produced by scan is always streamEnd.

      Proof strategy: Scanner.lean:1987 shows let final := final.emit .streamEnd followed immediately by return final.tokens (line 1988). The streamEnd token is the last modification before returning, so tokens[size-1] is streamEnd.

      Note: Complete proof requires showing no tokens are appended after streamEnd. Empirically validated on all test inputs (§4).

      §2 Position Monotonicity #

      Token positions (offsets) are monotonically non-decreasing throughout the token array. This follows from two properties:

      1. emit appends tokens without reordering (structural)
      2. Scanner operations either preserve offset or increase it (never decrease)
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.Array.getElem_congr {α : Type} {arr : Array α} {i j : Nat} (hi : i < arr.size) (hj : j < arr.size) (heq : i = j) :
      arr[i] = arr[j]

      Helper: array access is equal when indices are equal (regardless of proof terms).

      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.emit_preserves_position_order (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_ordered : ∀ (i j : Fin s.tokens.size), ↑i < ↑j → s.tokens[i].pos.offset ≤ s.tokens[j].pos.offset) (tok : YamlToken) (h_nonzero : s.tokens.size > 0) (h_pos : s.offset ≥ s.tokens[s.tokens.size - 1].pos.offset) (i j : Fin (s.emit tok).tokens.size) :
      ↑i < ↑j → (s.emit tok).tokens[i].pos.offset ≤ (s.emit tok).tokens[j].pos.offset
      source
      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.advance_increases_offset (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : s.hasMore = true) :
      s.advance.offset > s.offset

      The advance operation never decreases the offset.

      This is a direct consequence of string position advancement: moving forward in a UTF-8 string increases the byte offset by at least 1.

      Proof: Directly applies advance_offset_lt from ScannerProgress.lean.

      source
      def L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.ScanInv' (tokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)) (offset : Nat) :
      Prop

      The scanner maintains position monotonicity throughout its operation.

      Proof architecture:

      1. Define ScanInv s = tokens ordered ∧ all token offsets ≤ s.offset
      2. Prove ScanInv preserved by emit, advance, field updates, unwindIndents
      3. Express scanNextToken preservation as an axiom (empirically validated by 869 tests)
      4. Prove scanLoop ordering via induction on fuel
      5. Compose: initial state satisfies ScanInv, scanLoop preserves it

      Key insight: Within each scanNextToken iteration, ALL newly pushed tokens have the same offset (the post-skipToContent offset P₀). Since P₀ ≥ s.offset ≥ all existing token offsets, ordering is maintained. setIfInBounds (for simple key resolution) only overwrites placeholders with the same offset they were created at.

      Equations
      • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.
      Instances For
        source
        def L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.ScanInv (s : Scanner.ScannerState) :
        Prop
        Equations
        • L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.ScanInv s = L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.ScanInv' s.tokens s.offset
        Instances For

          ScanInv (s.emit tok)

          ScanInv s'
          source

          ScanInv s'
          source

          ScanInv (Scanner.unwindIndentsLoop s col fuel)

          ScanInv (Scanner.unwindIndents s col)

          Phase 1: Primitive ScanInv lemmas #

          Building blocks for the full scanNextToken_preserves_ScanInv proof.

          ScanInv (Scanner.scanValuePrepare s)

          theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.preprocess_unwind_preserves_ScanInv (s_skip : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_sinv : ScanInv s_skip) :
          ScanInv (if (!s_skip.inFlow && s_skip.needIndentCheck) = true then have __src := Scanner.unwindIndents s_skip ↑s_skip.col; { input := __src.input, inputEnd := __src.inputEnd, offset := __src.offset, line := __src.line, col := __src.col, indents := __src.indents, flowLevel := __src.flowLevel, tokens := __src.tokens, simpleKey := __src.simpleKey, simpleKeyStack := __src.simpleKeyStack, simpleKeyAllowed := __src.simpleKeyAllowed, needIndentCheck := false, allowDirectives := __src.allowDirectives, seenYamlDirective := __src.seenYamlDirective, directivesPresent := __src.directivesPresent, documentEverStarted := __src.documentEverStarted, flowStack := __src.flowStack, explicitKeyLine := __src.explicitKeyLine, comments := __src.comments, definedAnchors := __src.definedAnchors } else s_skip)
          source

          ScanInv s'

          theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.dispatchContent_preserves_ScanInv (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h : ScanInv s) (s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_ok : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchContent s c = Except.ok s') :
          ScanInv s'

          SimpleKeyValid: simpleKey bookkeeping invariant #

          When simpleKey.possible is true, the placeholder tokens at the saved indices have the correct position. This links simpleKey metadata to actual token array content and is needed by scanValuePrepare_preserves_ScanInv (which calls setIfInBounds at those indices).

          saveSimpleKey establishes this by pushing two placeholders at currentPos and recording tokenIndex = old_size, pos = currentPos. Between saveSimpleKey and scanValue, only token-appending operations occur, so the placeholders at the saved indices remain untouched.

          source
          def L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.SimpleKeyValid (s : Scanner.ScannerState) :
          Prop

          The simpleKey validity condition: when the simple key is possible, the saved tokenIndex and tokenIndex+1 are within bounds and the tokens at those positions have pos = simpleKey.pos.

          Equations
          • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.
          Instances For
            source
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.SimpleKeyValid_of_not_possible (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : s.simpleKey.possible = false) :
            SimpleKeyValid s
            source
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.SimpleKeyValid_of_cleared (_s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : s'.simpleKey.possible = false) :
            SimpleKeyValid s'
            source
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.SimpleKeyValid_mono (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_skv : SimpleKeyValid s) (h_sk : s'.simpleKey = s.simpleKey) (h_mono : s'.tokens.size ≥ s.tokens.size) (h_pref : ∀ (i : Nat) (h : i < s.tokens.size), s'.tokens[i] = s.tokens[i]) :
            SimpleKeyValid s'
            source
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.saveSimpleKey_preserves_SimpleKeyValid (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_skv : SimpleKeyValid s) :
            SimpleKeyValid (Scanner.saveSimpleKey s)

            SimpleKeyValid s'
            source

            SimpleKeyValid (Scanner.unwindIndents s col)

            SimpleKeyValid s'
            source
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.allowDir_ite_preserves_SimpleKeyValid (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_skv : SimpleKeyValid s) :
            SimpleKeyValid (if s.allowDirectives = true then { input := s.input, inputEnd := s.inputEnd, offset := s.offset, line := s.line, col := s.col, indents := s.indents, flowLevel := s.flowLevel, tokens := s.tokens, simpleKey := s.simpleKey, simpleKeyStack := s.simpleKeyStack, simpleKeyAllowed := s.simpleKeyAllowed, needIndentCheck := s.needIndentCheck, allowDirectives := false, seenYamlDirective := s.seenYamlDirective, directivesPresent := s.directivesPresent, documentEverStarted := true, flowStack := s.flowStack, explicitKeyLine := s.explicitKeyLine, comments := s.comments, definedAnchors := s.definedAnchors } else s)
            source
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.allowDir_ite_preserves_ScanInv (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_inv : ScanInv s) :
            ScanInv (if s.allowDirectives = true then { input := s.input, inputEnd := s.inputEnd, offset := s.offset, line := s.line, col := s.col, indents := s.indents, flowLevel := s.flowLevel, tokens := s.tokens, simpleKey := s.simpleKey, simpleKeyStack := s.simpleKeyStack, simpleKeyAllowed := s.simpleKeyAllowed, needIndentCheck := s.needIndentCheck, allowDirectives := false, seenYamlDirective := s.seenYamlDirective, directivesPresent := s.directivesPresent, documentEverStarted := true, flowStack := s.flowStack, explicitKeyLine := s.explicitKeyLine, comments := s.comments, definedAnchors := s.definedAnchors } else s)
            source
            theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.SimpleKeyValid_implies_scanValue_h_sk (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_skv : SimpleKeyValid s) :
            (Scanner.scanValueClearKey s).simpleKey.possible = true → (Scanner.scanValueClearKey s).simpleKey.tokenIndex < (Scanner.scanValueClearKey s).tokens.size ∧ (Scanner.scanValueClearKey s).simpleKey.tokenIndex + 1 < (Scanner.scanValueClearKey s).tokens.size ∧ (∀ (h1 : (Scanner.scanValueClearKey s).simpleKey.tokenIndex < (Scanner.scanValueClearKey s).tokens.size), (Scanner.scanValueClearKey s).tokens[(Scanner.scanValueClearKey s).simpleKey.tokenIndex].pos = (Scanner.scanValueClearKey s).simpleKey.pos) ∧ ∀ (h2 : (Scanner.scanValueClearKey s).simpleKey.tokenIndex + 1 < (Scanner.scanValueClearKey s).tokens.size), (Scanner.scanValueClearKey s).tokens[(Scanner.scanValueClearKey s).simpleKey.tokenIndex + 1].pos = (Scanner.scanValueClearKey s).simpleKey.pos

            SimpleKeyStackValid: validity of stacked simple keys #

            Flow collection openers ([, {) push the current simple key onto the stack. Closers (], }) restore it. Between push and pop, the token array only grows and existing entries are never modified (only extended). Therefore, the token-position equalities that held at push time continue to hold at pop time.

            source
            def L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.SimpleKeyStackValid (s : Scanner.ScannerState) :
            Prop

            Validity of every simple key entry on the stack.

            Equations
            • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.
            Instances For

              Prop

              Combined simple key validity for both current and stacked keys.

              Equations
              • L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.AllKeysValid s = (L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.SimpleKeyValid s ∧ L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.SimpleKeyStackValid s)

                AllKeysValid s'

                AllKeysValid s'

                AllKeysValid s'

                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.dispatchBlockIndicators_preserves_AllKeysValid (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchBlockIndicators s c = Except.ok (some s')) (h_akv : AllKeysValid s) :
                AllKeysValid s'
                source
                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.dispatchContent_preserves_AllKeysValid (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchContent s c = Except.ok s') (h_akv : AllKeysValid s) :
                AllKeysValid s'

                scanNextToken preserves AllKeysValid #

                The proof case-splits on which dispatcher handles the character:

                • Dispatchers that clear possible produce SimpleKeyValid vacuously.
                • Dispatchers that preserve simpleKey and only append tokens preserve it via monotonicity.
                • Flow-start pushes the valid current key onto the stack and clears current.
                • Flow-end restores a valid key from the stack.
                source
                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scanNextToken_postStructural_preserves_AllKeysValid (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_akv : AllKeysValid s) (h_ok : (match Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchFlowIndicators s c with | Except.ok (some s') => Except.ok (some s') | Except.ok none => match Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchBlockIndicators s c with | Except.ok (some s') => Except.ok (some s') | Except.ok none => Except.map some (Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchContent s c) | Except.error e => Except.error e | Except.error e => Except.error e) = Except.ok (some s')) :
                AllKeysValid s'
                source
                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scanNextToken_preserves_AllKeysValid (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) :
                AllKeysValid s → Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok (some s') → AllKeysValid s'

                scanNextToken preserves ScanInv #

                The proof composes all per-dispatcher preservation theorems. The SimpleKeyValid condition is carried as a side invariant to supply the h_sk precondition needed by scanValue_preserves_ScanInv.

                source
                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scanNextToken_postStructural_preserves_ScanInv (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h_inv : ScanInv s) (h_skv : SimpleKeyValid s) (h_ok : (match Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchFlowIndicators s c with | Except.ok (some s') => Except.ok (some s') | Except.ok none => match Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchBlockIndicators s c with | Except.ok (some s') => Except.ok (some s') | Except.ok none => Except.map some (Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchContent s c) | Except.error e => Except.error e | Except.error e => Except.error e) = Except.ok (some s')) :
                ScanInv s'
                source
                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scanNextToken_preserves_ScanInv (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) :
                ScanInv s → SimpleKeyValid s → Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok (some s') → ScanInv s'
                source
                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scanLoop_ordered (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (fuel : Nat) (tokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)) (h_inv : ScanInv s) (h_akv : AllKeysValid s) (h_ok : Scanner.scanLoop s fuel = Except.ok tokens) (i j : Fin tokens.size) :
                ↑i < ↑j → tokens[i].pos.offset ≤ tokens[j].pos.offset
                source
                theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scan_positions_ordered (input : String) (tokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)) (h : Scanner.scan input = Except.ok tokens) (i j : Fin tokens.size) :
                ↑i < ↑j → tokens[i].pos.offset ≤ tokens[j].pos.offset

                §3 Main Correctness Theorem #

                Composition of envelope and monotonicity properties establishes that scan produces ValidTokenStreams.

                source
                def L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scan_produces_valid_tokens (input : String) (tokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)) (h : Scanner.scan input = Except.ok tokens) :
                Grammar.ValidTokenStream

                Main result: The scanner produces valid token streams.

                Every successful scan produces a token array that satisfies all four ValidTokenStream invariants:

                1. At least 2 tokens (envelope)
                2. First token is streamStart
                3. Last token is streamEnd
                4. Token positions are monotonically non-decreasing

                This is a def (not theorem) because ValidTokenStream is a structure (data), not a proposition. The definition constructs the witness.

                Equations
                • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.
                Instances For

                  §3.5 scanFiltered Lifts ValidTokenStream #

                  Filtering out internal .placeholder tokens preserves all ValidTokenStream invariants (size, envelope, ordering). This bridges the scanner's internal representation to the token stream consumed by TokenParser.

                  source
                  def L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scanFiltered_produces_valid_tokens (input : String) (ftokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)) (h : Scanner.scanFiltered input = Except.ok ftokens) :
                  Grammar.ValidTokenStream

                  Filtering out placeholder tokens from a valid scan result preserves ValidTokenStream.

                  Equations
                  • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.
                  Instances For

                    §4 Compile-Time Verification #

                    #guard checks demonstrating the theorem on concrete inputs. These provide empirical validation before the universal proof.

                    §4.1 ValidTokenStreamProp Bridge Theorems #

                    ValidTokenStreamProp is the Prop twin of ValidTokenStream. These theorems make scanner correctness visible to the doc-verification-bridge, which classifies def-as-witness patterns as computationalOperation rather than theorems.

                    source
                    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scan_valid_token_stream (input : String) (tokens : Array (Positioned YamlToken)) (h : Scanner.scan input = Except.ok tokens) :
                    Grammar.ValidTokenStreamProp tokens

                    Prop-level scanner correctness: scan produces tokens satisfying all four ValidTokenStreamProp invariants.

                    source
                    theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.ValidTokenStream_iff_Prop (vts : Grammar.ValidTokenStream) :
                    Grammar.ValidTokenStreamProp vts.tokens

                    Projection: ValidTokenStream fields imply ValidTokenStreamProp.

                    source
                    def L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.checkValidStream (input : String) :
                    Bool
                    Equations
                    • One or more equations did not get rendered due to their size.
                    Instances For

                      §5 Scanner Progress — scanNextToken Advances Offset #

                      Goal: Prove that every successful scanNextToken call that returns some s' has s'.offset > s.offset. This is the key termination argument for ScanChain.fuel_bound.

                      Structure:

                      • §5.1: unwindIndents preserves offset/inputEnd/input
                      • §5.2: skipToContent pipeline offset monotonicity
                      • §5.3: scanNextToken_preprocess offset monotonicity + hasMore
                      • §5.4: Per-dispatch-branch offset strict inequality
                      • §5.5: scanNextToken_progress capstone

                      §5.1 unwindIndents Preserves offset/inputEnd/input #

                      unwindIndentsLoop only calls emit .blockEnd and pops indents. Neither operation touches offset, inputEnd, or input.

                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.unwindIndentsLoop_offset_eq (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (col : Int) (fuel : Nat) :
                      (Scanner.unwindIndentsLoop s col fuel).offset = s.offset
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.unwindIndents_offset_eq (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (col : Int) :
                      (Scanner.unwindIndents s col).offset = s.offset
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.unwindIndentsLoop_inputEnd_eq (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (col : Int) (fuel : Nat) :
                      (Scanner.unwindIndentsLoop s col fuel).inputEnd = s.inputEnd
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.unwindIndents_inputEnd_eq (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (col : Int) :
                      (Scanner.unwindIndents s col).inputEnd = s.inputEnd
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.unwindIndentsLoop_input_eq (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (col : Int) (fuel : Nat) :
                      (Scanner.unwindIndentsLoop s col fuel).input = s.input
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.unwindIndents_input_eq (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (col : Int) :
                      (Scanner.unwindIndents s col).input = s.input

                      §5.2 skipToContent Pipeline Offset Monotonicity #

                      Each phase of the skipToContent pipeline preserves or increases offset:

                      • skipToContentWs: may advance past whitespace (offset ≥)
                      • skipToContentComment: may advance past comment (offset ≥)
                      • consumeNewline: may advance past newline (offset ≥)
                      • skipToContentLoop: composition, monotone by induction
                      • skipToContent: trivial wrapper
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.skipToContentWs_offset_ge (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : Scanner.skipToContentWs s = Except.ok s') :
                      s'.offset ≥ s.offset
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.skipToContentComment_offset_ge (s : Scanner.ScannerState) :
                      (Scanner.skipToContentComment s).offset ≥ s.offset
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.skipToContentLoop_offset_ge (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (fuel : Nat) (h : Scanner.skipToContentLoop s fuel = Except.ok s') :
                      s'.offset ≥ s.offset
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.skipToContent_offset_ge (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : Scanner.skipToContent s = Except.ok s') :
                      s'.offset ≥ s.offset

                      §5.3 scanNextToken_preprocess Offset Monotonicity #

                      scanNextToken_preprocess runs skipToContent + unwindIndents + saveSimpleKey. All three preserve or increase offset. When it returns some (s', c), s'.peek? = some c, which means s'.hasMore.

                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.preprocess_offset_ge (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_preprocess s = Except.ok (some (s', c))) :
                      s'.offset ≥ s.offset
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.preprocess_hasMore (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_preprocess s = Except.ok (some (s', c))) :
                      s'.offset < s'.inputEnd

                      §5.3.5 Sub-Scanner Offset Progress (Strict) #

                      Each sub-scanner called by the dispatch functions strictly advances offset. These use advance_offset_lt from ScannerProgress for the first advance, and the _offset_ge helpers from §A for subsequent operations.

                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.svck_offset (s : Scanner.ScannerState) :
                      (Scanner.scanValueClearKey s).offset = s.offset
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.svck_inputEnd (s : Scanner.ScannerState) :
                      (Scanner.scanValueClearKey s).inputEnd = s.inputEnd
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.svp_offset (s : Scanner.ScannerState) :
                      (Scanner.scanValuePrepare s).offset = s.offset
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.svp_inputEnd (s : Scanner.ScannerState) :
                      (Scanner.scanValuePrepare s).inputEnd = s.inputEnd
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scanValue_offset_lt (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (hlt : s.offset < s.inputEnd) (h : Scanner.scanValue s = Except.ok s') :
                      s.offset < s'.offset

                      scanValue strictly advances offset when offset < inputEnd.

                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scanDocumentStart_offset_lt (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (hlt : s.offset < s.inputEnd) :
                      s.offset < (Scanner.scanDocumentStart s).offset

                      scanDocumentStart strictly advances offset when offset < inputEnd.

                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.docEnd_core (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (hlt : s.offset < s.inputEnd) (s_u : Scanner.ScannerState) :
                      Scanner.unwindIndents s (-1) = s_u → s.offset < (({ input := s_u.input, inputEnd := s_u.inputEnd, offset := s_u.offset, line := s_u.line, col := s_u.col, indents := s_u.indents, flowLevel := s_u.flowLevel, tokens := s_u.tokens, simpleKeyStack := s_u.simpleKeyStack, simpleKeyAllowed := s_u.simpleKeyAllowed, needIndentCheck := s_u.needIndentCheck, allowDirectives := s_u.allowDirectives, seenYamlDirective := s_u.seenYamlDirective, directivesPresent := s_u.directivesPresent, documentEverStarted := s_u.documentEverStarted, flowStack := s_u.flowStack, explicitKeyLine := s_u.explicitKeyLine, comments := s_u.comments, definedAnchors := s_u.definedAnchors }.emit YamlToken.documentEnd).advanceN 3).offset
                      source

                      s.offset < s'.offset

                      source

                      s'.offset ≥ s.offset

                      s.offset < s'.offset

                      §5.4 Per-Dispatch Offset Strict Inequality #

                      Each dispatch function that returns some s' calls advance at least once on a state with hasMore, giving s'.offset > s_in.offset.

                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.dispatchStructural_offset_gt (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h_hm : s.offset < s.inputEnd) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchStructural s c = Except.ok (some s')) :
                      s'.offset > s.offset
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.dispatchFlowIndicators_offset_gt (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h_hm : s.offset < s.inputEnd) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchFlowIndicators s c = Except.ok (some s')) :
                      s'.offset > s.offset
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.dispatchBlockIndicators_offset_gt (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h_hm : s.offset < s.inputEnd) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchBlockIndicators s c = Except.ok (some s')) :
                      s'.offset > s.offset
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.dispatchContent_offset_gt (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h_hm : s.offset < s.inputEnd) (hpeek : s.peek? = some c) (hnoDoc : (s.col == 0 && Scanner.atDocumentBoundary s) = false) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchContent s c = Except.ok s') :
                      s'.offset > s.offset

                      §5.5 scanNextToken_progress — Capstone #

                      Every successful scanNextToken call returning some s' has s'.offset > s.offset. This is the key termination argument.

                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.dispatchStructural_none_noDoc (s : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_dispatchStructural s c = Except.ok none) :
                      (s.col == 0 && Scanner.atDocumentBoundary s) = false
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.preprocess_peek_eq (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (c : Char) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken_preprocess s = Except.ok (some (s', c))) :
                      s'.peek? = some c
                      source
                      theorem L4YAML.Proofs.ScannerCorrectness.scanNextToken_progress (s s' : Scanner.ScannerState) (h : Scanner.scanNextToken s = Except.ok (some s')) :
                      s'.offset > s.offset
